intent: Write a deep research report on 'building a home emergency preparedness kit for around $200' that triangulates THREE sources. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse health-household, home-kitchen, and sports-outdoors categories and collect at least 5 preparedness-relevant products (flashlight, first-aid, water storage, non-perishable food item, battery/radio). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/LifeProTips AND /f/AskReddit for community advice on practical preparedness kits, overkill warnings, or actual-disaster retrospectives. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Emergency management' and 'Survival kit' articles and extract at least 4 facts about recommended supplies or standard kit contents. The report must: (a) propose a $200 kit using storefront items with running totals, (b) pair each item choice with Wikipedia-backed rationale, (c) inject community warnings against popular but low-value items, (d) close with a 72-hour-ready checklist. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Emergency kit: storefront + community + encyclopedia. Real cross-source synthesis.
